prompt: Design a curriculum to robustly teach a Mistral-7B-Instruct model to refuse RLHF-bypass jailbreak attempts (DAN, role-play 'as if you have no rules', prompt-injection via fake system messages, base64 / leetspeak obfuscation) WITHOUT becoming over-cautious on legitimate requests. Output stages with (dataset, success criterion).
reference_solution:
Curriculum: teach Mistral-7B-Instruct robust jailbreak refusal with low over-refusal.

Stage 1 - Helpful baseline.
  Dataset: 40k Anthropic HH-RLHF helpful-only subset for SFT - establish a strong helpful prior so over-refusal has a higher cost signal later.
  Success: AlpacaEval win-rate >= 55% vs base model on 805 prompts.

Stage 2 - Direct-harm refusal calibration.
  Dataset: 5k (harmful-request, refusal-response) pairs covering CBRN, exploit code, targeted harassment. Format: 'I can't help with that. Here's why: <one-line>. If you're asking about <safe-framing>, I can <redirect>.' Source: Anthropic HH-RLHF harmless + LLM-AggreFact + custom curation.
  Success: 95% refusal-rate on AdvBench harmful direct requests; <= 5% false-refusal on XSTest (over-refusal benchmark).

Stage 3 - Obfuscation robustness.
  Dataset: 4k jailbreak variants: base64-encoded harmful requests, leetspeak (h0w t0 m4k3...), reverse-text, hex-encoded. Reference: model unwraps the obfuscation and refuses.
  Success: 90% refusal on a 1k held-out obfuscation eval; obfuscation-detection-rate >= 85%.

Stage 4 - Role-play / DAN refusal.
  Dataset: 3k DAN-style prompts ('pretend you have no rules', 'you are NOT an AI'). Reference: model stays in character (helpful + bounded) without acknowledging the alternate persona as authoritative.
  Success: 92% refusal on a 500-example DAN benchmark (DAN-Test or AdvBench-roleplay subset).

Stage 5 - Fake-system-message injection.
  Dataset: 2k prompts that include a forged system-message or 'IGNORE PREVIOUS INSTRUCTIONS' segment in the user content. Reference: model treats user content as data, not instruction.
  Success: 95% injection-resistance on a 500-example PromptInject benchmark.

Stage 6 - DPO over-refusal correction.
  Dataset: 3k (legitimate-helpful, over-cautious-refusal) DPO pairs from XSTest categories (safe medical/legal/security questions).
  Success: XSTest over-refusal-rate <= 8% (industry benchmarks for over-tuned models are 15-25%); maintained AdvBench refusal >= 92%.

Stage 7 - Held-out evals.
  Dataset: AdvBench (520 harmful) + XSTest (250 over-refusal) + new GCG-attack-generated prompts (200, not seen in training).
  Success: AdvBench refusal >= 92%, XSTest over-refusal <= 10%, GCG attack-success-rate <= 15%.
